frameworks.name: resource-rational analysis
frameworks.one_line: rational trade-off between reward and bounded cognitive resources

frameworks.name: policy compression
frameworks.one_line: capacity limit applies to action-selection channels

frameworks.name: expected utility maximization
frameworks.one_line: standard rationality view that symptoms appear to violate

core_question: Can aspects of psychopathology be explained as resource-rational trade-offs rather than failures of rationality?
key_assumptions: agents trade off reward against cognitive capacity
capacity limits act on perceptual and action channels
apparent irrationality may reflect optimal bounded computation
manipulations: review-level contrast across affective, psychotic, and neurodevelopmental disorders
comparison of effects of psychoactive medications across disorders
key_findings: symptoms across diverse disorders fit resource-rational predictions
rational inattention parsimoniously accounts for several perceptual symptoms
policy compression accounts for action-selection symptoms
psychoactive medication effects align with shifts in capacity trade-offs
interpretations: some psychopathological symptoms reflect rational resource trade-offs, not broken rationality
capacity-limited rationality unifies affective, psychotic, and neurodevelopmental phenomena
